mnemonics.title: PROBABILITY Axioms
mnemonics.content: All Probability values Range between 0 and 1. Sample space always has probability 1. For mutually exclusive events, add them up.

mnemonics.title: Bayes' Theorem
mnemonics.content: Posterior = Likelihood × Prior / Evidence. Like: P(A|B) = [P(B|A) × P(A)] / P(B). Think: 'Evidence updates belief'.

mnemonics.title: Independent vs Mutually Exclusive
mnemonics.content: Independent: 'A doesn't care about B' (multiplication works). Mutually Exclusive: 'A and B cannot happen together' (intersection is zero).

mnemonics.title: Addition Rule
mnemonics.content: Union = Sum - Intersection. Remember Venn diagram overlap. For 'OR' think ADD then SUBTRACT overlap.

acronyms.expansion: Sample Space - set of all possible outcomes
acronyms.usage: S = {H, T} for coin toss, S = {1,2,3,4,5,6} for die
E = {2,4,6} for 'even number' when rolling die

acronyms.expansion: Probability of A OR B occurring (Union)
acronyms.usage: P(even OR odd) = 1 for die roll

acronyms.expansion: Probability of A AND B occurring (Intersection)
Probability of A given B has occurred (Conditional)

visualTips.visualization: Draw two overlapping circles for A and B. The overlap is A∩B. Total union area = A + B - overlap. For mutually exclusive, circles don't overlap.
Think of probability as a number line from 0 (impossible) to 1 (certain). 0.5 is exactly halfway - like fair coin. 0.1 is rare, 0.9 is likely.
Draw branches: First event (with probabilities), then second event branches from each first event. Multiply along path for joint probability.
P(A|B) = (portion of B that belongs to A) / (total B). Visualize as: slice B from the whole, what fraction is A?

analogies.analogy: Like a menu listing ALL possible dishes at a restaurant - every outcome you could possibly get.
Your order from that menu - a specific subset like 'all vegetarian dishes'.
Two separate coin tosses - result of first doesn't influence second. Like two different people flipping coins in different rooms.
Being both single and married at the same time - impossible. Events that cannot happen together.
Updating your guess after getting new information. Like: 'What's the chance it's raining?' (prior) vs 'What's the chance it's raining given you see dark clouds?' (posterior).
